calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-13-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Erika M. Haraguchi for Anita Wu, Petitioner/Administrator.

The court inquired about the good cause basis for accepting the petitioner's spousal election beyond the four-month period. Ms. Haraguchi explained that her client chose not to file a spousal property petition due to the significant creditors' claims against the estate. The client sought to keep the assets separate from her late husband's estate to avoid liability for those claims. Additionally, early in the case, there was uncertainty regarding the existence of a will, as a friend of the decedent had suggested one might be produced. To ensure clarity in probate proceedings, the petitioner opted for a full probate process instead of claiming spousal property.

The court found good cause to accept the late spousal election. Given that the only other interested party-the decedent's adult son-filed a waiver of accounting and raised no objections, the court took the petition under submission with intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
